title: The Vain Jackdaw
content:
"Fine feathers," Jack thought, "make fine fowls;
    I'll be envied of bats & of owls:"
    But the peacocks' proud eyes
    Saw through his disguise,
    And Jack fled the assembly of fowls.

    Borrowed Plumes Are Soon Discovered
meaning:
Walter Crane's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Vain Jackdaw"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
